codeReview.summary: Implementation is correct and fully covers the feature requirements. ConfidenceLevel enum with 4 threshold zones (Deprecated<0.3, Uncertain 0.3-0.6, Probable 0.6-0.8, Confident>0.8) is properly implemented with Bayesian posterior tracking via BetaPosterior. Helper methods (has_sufficient_data, total_observations) added. All 11 test cases pass covering threshold boundaries, zone transitions, serialization, and API methods.

issues.description: Floating point precision edge case: values like 0.30000000000000004 (slightly above 0.3 due to float precision) would classify as Deprecated when they should be Uncertain. This is a pre-existing risk in floating-point comparisons, not introduced by this feature.
NaN handling: from_probability(NaN) returns Confident (the default case) since NaN < 0.3 is false. This edge case behavior may be unexpected but is not likely to occur in normal operation.

Documentation ambiguity: The max_probability() method returns 0.6 for Uncertain, but since is_met_by uses exclusive ranges (0.3..0.6), a probability of exactly 0.6 would NOT be considered Uncertain. This is correct behavior but could be clarified in documentation.

summary: Feature mem-confidence-bayesian passes scrutiny. The Alpha/Beta posterior tracking with 0.3-0.6-0.8 threshold zones is correctly implemented. ConfidenceLevel enum, confidence_level() methods on BetaPosterior and Procedure, and helper methods (has_sufficient_data, total_observations) all work as specified. All 11 tests pass including threshold boundary tests, zone transition tests, and serialization tests. Build and clippy pass with no warnings. No blocking issues found. Minor floating-point precision and NaN handling edge cases noted but not actionable.
